page: Amazon Web Services c5a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The CPU allocation is dedicated, supported by 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, which equates to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance utilizes the Nitro hypervisor, includes no local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the c5a.4xlarge delivers average multi-core CPU performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth also presents a bottleneck under large, uncached workloads; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average, uncached large-block read and write bandwidths fall into the bottom 25%.

Given its compute-focused configuration and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is designed for compute-heavy workloads that benefit from multi-threaded execution rather than high memory density or local storage. It achieves average throughput in static web serving, Redis SET operations, and software compilation. Qualitatively, the c5a.4xlarge offers a cost-efficient option within the compute-optimized class by leveraging AMD EPYC processors to deliver balanced multi-core performance without the premium associated with high-capacity local storage or memory-heavy configurations. Best-fit workloads include web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, and moderate software compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c5a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, this server provides dedicated CPU resources at 3.3 GHz but lacks local storage, relying on network-attached storage and a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core CPU capabilities and standard memory bandwidth for cached operations, though single-core performance and uncached memory bandwidth represent clear tradeoffs, ranking in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. This resource profile makes the instance a cost-efficient option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios. It is suited for applications such as static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R32 CPU, delivering average multi-core performance for web serving and compilation.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.3 GHz AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a balanced multi-core profile for compute-focused workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores
32.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB per core
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and Nitro hypervisor virtualization
Average multi-core CPU and software compilation benchmark performance
Tradeoffs in single-core execution and uncached memory bandwidth
Suited for static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and general compute tasks
